title: Java Static Members
description: Practice static constants and static helper methods.

choices: Compilation fails because scale is not static
completionChecks.explanation: scale multiplies its argument by the static constant SCALE (10), so MathBox.scale(5) returns 50; both SCALE and scale belong to the class, not an instance.

completionChecks.prompt: How is SCALE shared in MathBox?
choices: As a separate field on each MathBox instance
As a local variable inside scale only
As one class-level static final field shared by all callers
As a new copy created on every scale call
completionChecks.explanation: static attaches SCALE to the class so a single shared copy exists, and final prevents reassignment after initialization.

completionChecks.prompt: What happens if scale tries to run SCALE = 20; before returning?
choices: The method returns value * 20 for that call only
The method changes SCALE for every MathBox instance
Compilation fails because SCALE is final
An UnsupportedOperationException is thrown at runtime
completionChecks.explanation: SCALE is a static final field initialized once, and assigning to it inside the static method is a compile-time error.
